# Lerian Midaz

> Source-available (Elastic License 2.0) core banking platform in a single Go 1.26.3 monorepo (v4 module path, single root go.mod — no go.work) on lib-commons/v5 v5.4.1 + lib-observability v1.0.1. One repository, five deploy units: a unified Ledger HTTP API (double-entry onboarding + transaction + CRM holders/instruments + fees) on :3002; a Tracer real-time transaction-validation / fraud-prevention API on :4020; a two-unit Reporter (manager :4005 + worker :4006 health-only consumer); and an Infra docker-compose backing stack. Stores: PostgreSQL (primary/replica), MongoDB (metadata + CRM + fees), RabbitMQ (async transactions, reporter jobs), Valkey/Redis (caching), and SeaweedFS (reporter object storage). Multi-tenant isolation via the lib-commons v5 tenant-manager.

Midaz is no longer just a ledger: the monorepo now ships a complete, composable core banking platform — a double-entry ledger plus real-time transaction validation/fraud prevention, async reporting, CRM, and a fee engine, all building from the single root `go.mod`. The Ledger deploy unit (`:3002`) is one binary that folds four domains: onboarding (org/ledger/asset/portfolio/segment/account management), transaction processing (n:n double-entry with DSL, JSON, inflow/outflow, annotation modes), **CRM** (holders/instruments, served on the same port under the host ledger's `midaz` namespace, scoped by the `X-Organization-Id` header), and **fees** (fee engine + billing packages, applied during transaction create). CRM and fees are NOT separate services — CRM is a package tree (`components/crm`) imported by the ledger binary, and fees are embedded in it. Tracer, Reporter Manager, and Reporter Worker are separate Go service deploy units. Infrastructure is provisioned via a single Docker Compose (PostgreSQL 17, MongoDB, RabbitMQ 4.1, Valkey, SeaweedFS, KEDA, Grafana/OTEL-LGTM). Transactional messaging (PIX, cards, wires) and governance integrations remain external/marketplace.

The financial hierarchy: Organizations → Ledgers → Assets, Portfolios, Segments → Accounts → Transactions/Operations → Balances.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): Project overview, architecture, core banking platform vision
- [License](LICENSE): Elastic License 2.0 (source-available)
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): Release history (single unified monorepo version)

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Universal AI agent entry point — quick start, architecture, conventions, what not to do
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Deep reference for AI agents — code patterns, interfaces, commands, configuration, debugging
- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): Complete reference — env vars, API endpoints, error codes, models

## Platform Overview

The five deploy units form one core banking platform in one monorepo:

- [Ledger](STRUCTURE.md): Double-entry ledger — the unified binary on :3002 (onboarding + transaction + CRM + fees)
- [Tracer](components/tracer/): Real-time transaction validation / fraud prevention on :4020 — CEL rules, multi-scope spending limits, hash-chained audit
- [Reporter Manager](components/reporter-manager/): Async report generation API on :4005 → RabbitMQ jobs (worker :4006 health-only consumer renders to S3-compatible storage)
- [CRM](components/crm/): Holders + instruments, folded into the ledger binary (not a deploy unit)
- [Fees](components/ledger/pkg/fee): Fee engine + billing packages, embedded in the ledger binary

## API

- [Ledger OpenAPI Spec](components/ledger/api/): Swagger for ledger, transaction, CRM holder/instrument, and fee endpoints (single folded spec; swag-fold source)
- [Per-Component OpenAPI Specs](postman/specs/): published `swagger.json`/`swagger.yaml`/`openapi.yaml` per REST deploy unit (ledger, tracer, reporter-manager)
- [RBAC Namespaces](docs/auth/RBAC-NAMESPACES.md): The three authz namespaces (`midaz`, `routing`, `plugin-fees`) in the unified binary (R9)
- [API Scoping](docs/api/SCOPING.md): Path-based vs `X-Organization-Id` header scoping conventions (R22)

## Architecture

- [Ledger Component](components/ledger/): Unified deploy unit — onboarding + transaction + CRM + fees in one process (:3002)
  - [Bootstrap](components/ledger/internal/bootstrap/): Composition root — config, DI, server lifecycle, `initCRM`, fee wiring, multi-tenant middleware
  - [HTTP Handlers](components/ledger/internal/adapters/http/in/): Fiber handlers, routes, middleware, `fees_routes.go`, CRM handlers (`crm_routes.go`, `composition_routes.go`, `holder.go`, `holder_accounts.go`, `instrument.go`)
  - [PostgreSQL Repos](components/ledger/internal/adapters/postgres/): Onboarding + transaction repositories
  - [MongoDB Repos](components/ledger/internal/adapters/mongodb/): Metadata + fees storage
  - [Services](components/ledger/internal/services/): CQRS command/query use cases + embedded `fees/`
  - [Embedded Fees](components/ledger/pkg/fee): `fee/` engine + [`feeshared/`](components/ledger/pkg/feeshared) (`plugin-fees`)
  - [Migrations](components/ledger/migrations/): SQL migrations for onboarding and transaction databases
- [CRM Package Tree](components/crm/): Holder/instrument domain imported by ledger — NOT a deploy unit
  - [MongoDB Repos](components/crm/adapters/mongodb/): CRM persistence (holder/ + instrument/)
  - [Services](components/crm/services/): CRM business logic
  - The entire CRM HTTP surface (handlers + routes) lives in the ledger tree (`components/ledger/internal/adapters/http/in/`, `midaz` namespace) so the swagger fold discovers it
- [Tracer Component](components/tracer/): Real-time validation / fraud-prevention API deploy unit (:4020) — CEL rules, spending limits, hash-chained audit
- [Reporter Manager](components/reporter-manager/): Report-request REST API deploy unit (:4005) → RabbitMQ jobs
- [Reporter Worker](components/reporter-worker/): Async report renderer deploy unit (:4006 health-only consumer) → S3-compatible storage
- [Infrastructure](components/infra/): Single Docker Compose for PostgreSQL, MongoDB, RabbitMQ, Valkey, SeaweedFS, KEDA, OTEL
- [Shared Packages](pkg/): Cross-component libraries
  - [Domain Models](pkg/mmodel/): Organization, Ledger, Account, Asset, Transaction, Balance, Holder, Instrument, etc.
  - [Error Constants](pkg/constant/errors.go): Ledger numeric codes (0001–0178, gap at 0130, incl. overdraft 0167–0175) + 16 CRM-00xx domain codes
  - [Error Types](pkg/errors.go): Typed errors — EntityNotFound, Validation, Conflict, Unauthorized, Forbidden, etc.
  - [Gold DSL](pkg/gold/): ANTLR4 grammar and parser for the purpose-built (Gold) transaction DSL
  - [Transaction Utilities](pkg/mtransaction/): Transaction validation and processing helpers
  - [Reporter Library](pkg/reporter/): Datasource/fetcher, rendering, storage — shared by both reporter units
  - [Streaming](pkg/streaming/): lib-streaming CloudEvents event modeling
  - [HTTP Utilities](pkg/net/http/): Middleware, pagination, protected routes, response helpers

## Configuration

- [Ledger .env.example](components/ledger/.env.example): Ledger env (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, RabbitMQ, OTEL, streaming, multi-tenant) + folded CRM (`MONGO_CRM_*`) and fees (`MONGO_FEES_*`)
- [Tracer .env.example](components/tracer/.env.example): Tracer env (own PostgreSQL, API key, OTEL)
- [Reporter Manager .env.example](components/reporter-manager/.env.example): Reporter manager env (MongoDB, RabbitMQ)
- [Reporter Worker .env.example](components/reporter-worker/.env.example): Reporter worker env (RabbitMQ, object storage, Chromium)
- [Infra .env.example](components/infra/.env.example): Infrastructure environment variables
- [Linter Config](.golangci.yml): golangci-lint v2.4.0 configuration
- [Infra Docker Compose](components/infra/docker-compose.yml): Full backing stack (single source)
